description: Harden MCP server HTTP responses for compatibility with Claude Code's MCP SDK auth flow. The first PRD (oauth-discovery-compat) fixed JSON 404 for catch-all and well-known routes. This follow-up addresses two remaining issues: (1) GET /mcp without a session returns 400 plain text, which the SDK throws as a StreamableHTTPError — should return 405 so the SDK silently proceeds; (2) the 401 response from checkBearerAuth lacks a WWW-Authenticate header, which is required by HTTP spec and helps the SDK distinguish Bearer auth from OAuth.

userStories.title: Return 405 for GET /mcp without a session ID
userStories.description: Change the GET /mcp handler to return 405 Method Not Allowed (instead of 400) when no mcp-session-id header is present. The MCP SDK treats 405 as 'server does not offer SSE stream' and silently proceeds, while 400 throws a StreamableHTTPError that surfaces as an error to the client.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /mcp with valid Bearer auth but no mcp-session-id returns 405
GET /mcp with a valid mcp-session-id still returns the SSE stream (existing behavior unchanged)
GET /mcp with an invalid mcp-session-id returns 400 (existing behavior for bad sessions unchanged)

userStories.title: Add WWW-Authenticate header to 401 responses
userStories.description: Add a `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` header to the 401 response returned by checkBearerAuth. This is required by RFC 7235 and helps MCP SDK clients distinguish Bearer token auth from OAuth.
acceptanceCriteria: 401 responses from /mcp include WWW-Authenticate: Bearer header
401 responses from other authenticated endpoints (/reload, /health, etc.) include WWW-Authenticate: Bearer header
The header value is 'Bearer' with no additional parameters

userStories.title: E2E tests for MCP auth hardening
userStories.description: Add E2E tests verifying the 405 response for GET /mcp without session, the WWW-Authenticate header on 401 responses, and the full connection flow simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies GET /mcp with Bearer auth but no session returns 405 (not 400)
Test verifies GET /mcp with valid session ID still returns 200 SSE stream
Test verifies DELETE /mcp without session returns 405
Test verifies 401 response includes WWW-Authenticate: Bearer header
Test simulates full MCP SDK connection: POST initialize → POST notification → GET SSE with session → all succeed
Test verifies GET /mcp without session does NOT trigger error in a realistic client flow
